blurb: Background Security Improvements enable Apple to ship rapid security remediations – including XProtect signature updates, runtime mitigations, and new security policies – independently of full OS releases. The mechanism uses cryptographically signed disk images called cryptexes (Cryptographic Extensions), which are verified and mounted at boot alongside the main system volume. Cryptexes carry their own Sealed System Volume hash tree guarantees and are delivered via standard software update infrastructure without requiring a full OS upgrade, allowing Apple to address newly discovered vulnerabilities with minimal user friction.
